rows.chunk: Today we are presenting our final ENES 104 group reflection. Our group members are Antranig Tatarian, Joshua Fawole, and Natneal Belete.
Our goal is to explain the main lessons we took from the course speakers and how those lessons changed the way we think about engineering, professionalism, and teamwork.
Across the semester, a few themes stood out to us as a group: professionalism, problem-solving, team collaboration, perseverance in industry, and the pursuit of knowledge.
These ideas kept showing up in different speakers' stories, even when they came from different industries or career paths.
For us, the biggest point was that success in engineering is not only about technical ability. It also depends on how you communicate, adapt, learn, and contribute to a team.
rows.reason: No strong deterministic faculty move was found.

rows.chunk: For my personal reflection, the speaker who stood out most was Dr. Joseph Varghese because he gave a very real perspective on working in Silicon Valley and in industry overall.

rows.chunk: One idea I took from him was the importance of interviewing the interviewer. Instead of only trying to impress a company, you also need to understand whether that environment is the right place for you to grow.

rows.chunk: Another lesson was that industry often works with a 'good enough' standard. That does not mean doing poor work; it means knowing when something meets the real requirement instead of chasing perfection forever.
